title: Deploy + locally smoke the chatui-aligned widget renderer (Claude wrote the code; you ship to kind, validate, bump pointers)
description: Claude wrote the widget renderer for AI-OS round 2 in repos/gui — every chatui app:* widget (32 kinds) plus three NoETL extensions (app:code, app:iframe, app:link). Renderer is a switch dispatcher with class ErrorBoundary, modeled on mlflowio/chatui's MessageContent.tsx. Args field names match chatui verbatim. NoetlPrompt's PromptEntry gains optional render?: WidgetContent; the report command surfaces widgets via the new extractAgentRender helper in services/agentResult.ts. handleWidgetEvent bridges chatui WidgetMessageEvent into prompt commands. Local TypeScript check (tsc --noEmit) passes clean. NO noetl python changes. NO catalog kind changes. Files are in the working tree, NOT committed. Codex job: build/lint, open + merge gui PR + docs PR, redeploy to local kind via ops/automation/development/noetl.yaml, smoke the widget renderer with a synthetic playbook, bump ai-meta pointers. Cap: 2 PRs.

1_typecheck_and_build.objective: Confirm the GUI compiles cleanly with the 35 new widget components.
spec: `cd repos/gui && npm install && npx tsc --noEmit` — Claude already verified this passes locally; capture the actual exit code.

`cd repos/gui && npm run build` — confirm the production bundle still builds. Watch for: missing imports (AppCalendar uses dayjs which antd ships; AppIcon uses '@ant-design/icons'), TypeScript errors on the discriminated union, unused-warning noise on the widget catch-all type.
If build fails, capture the exact errors in the result file and STOP — Claude will design the fix in the next round. Don't try to debug TypeScript issues autonomously beyond obvious typos.
If the bundle size delta exceeds +60 KB gzip, flag it. Round 2 adds ~35 components plus types; ~30 KB gzip is the rough expected ceiling.
1_typecheck_and_build.verify: tsc --noEmit exits 0; vite build exits 0; bundle size delta captured.

2_open_and_merge_gui_pr.objective: Branch, commit, push, PR, merge the GUI changes.
spec: `cd repos/gui && git checkout -b kadyapam/widgets-chatui-aligned-renderer`.
Stage the 39 new files in src/components/widgets/ plus the 2 modified files (NoetlPrompt.tsx, services/agentResult.ts).

Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc to dodge zsh history expansion. Message body explains: AI-OS round 2, chatui-aligned widget renderer, 32 chatui kinds + 3 NoETL extensions, NoetlPrompt PromptEntry.render, references to mlflowio/chatui pattern, references the design doc in ai-meta sync/issues/2026-05-08-noetl-as-ai-os-token-architecture.md.
PR title: `feat(gui): chatui-aligned widget renderer for NoetlPrompt (AI-OS round 2)`.
Push, PR, merge with `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch`. Auto-merge isn't enabled on noetl/gui per recent precedent — be ready to merge manually after CI passes.
Watch for any noetl/gui CI checks; fix in-flight if a lint/typecheck fails on the PR.
2_open_and_merge_gui_pr.verify: PR merged in noetl/gui. Capture PR URL + merge SHA + new release tag.

3_open_and_merge_docs_pr.objective: Branch, commit, push, PR, merge the widgets doc.
spec: `cd repos/docs && git checkout -b kadyapam/widgets-doc`.
Stage `docs/gui/widgets.md` (new file).

Verify build: `cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build`. The new file should be picked up by the GUI category's autogenerated sidebar — no sidebar.ts changes needed since `docs/gui/` is autogenerated.
If `npm run build` reports broken cross-links, fix them — the doc references `gui/catalog-ux.md`, `architecture/agent_orchestration.md`, and `mlflowio/chatui` (external link, fine).
Commit. PR title: `docs: widget rendering contract (chatui-aligned, app:* discriminator)`.
Push, PR, merge.
3_open_and_merge_docs_pr.verify: Docs PR merged. `npm run build` clean. Widgets page appears in the GUI category sidebar.

4_local_kind_redeploy.objective: Deploy the new GUI image to local kind via the configured ops playbook.
spec: Per AGENTS.md: local kind deploys MUST use the configured Podman machine only — no Colima, no Docker fallback. If Podman is unavailable or unhealthy, stop and report the Podman error.
After noetl/gui's PR merges, semantic-release CI cuts a new image tag (e.g. v1.8.0 — let CI pick, don't predict).
Wait for the release: `gh -R noetl/gui release view --json tagName -q .tagName` until the new tag is published.

Redeploy via the canonical ops playbook (per AGENTS.md): `cd repos/ops && noetl run automation/development/noetl.yaml --runtime local --set action=redeploy --set noetl_repo_dir=../noetl` — this rebuilds noetl + gui images and reapplies the helm chart against the kind cluster.

If the canonical playbook doesn't bump the gui tag specifically, fall back to: `noetl exec noetl/lifecycle/bump_image --runtime local --payload '{"deployment":"gui","namespace":"noetl","image":"ghcr.io/noetl/gui:<TAG>"}'`.
Wait for rollout: `kubectl --context kind-noetl -n noetl rollout status deploy/gui --timeout=180s`.
Confirm gui pod is running on the new tag and is Ready.
4_local_kind_redeploy.verify: Kind gui deployment is on the new release tag, pod healthy, no CrashLoopBackoff.

5_widget_smoke_via_synthetic_playbook.objective: Validate the widget renderer surfaces a real {render: {type, args}} payload through the report command.
spec:
Register a tiny smoke playbook in the kind catalog. Suggested path: `tests/spike/widget_render_smoke`. The playbook has one python step that returns `{ok: True, render: {type: 'app:column', args: {children: [{type: 'app:alert', args: {message: 'widget renderer is live', variant: 'success'}}, {type: 'app:markdown', args: {text: '## Widget smoke

* alert above
* markdown here
* `code` works

[noetl docs](https://docs.noetl.dev)'}}, {type: 'app:button', args: {text: 'reopen execution', variant: 'primary', event: {key: 'command', value: 'report ${execution_id}'}}}]}}`.
Run the playbook end-to-end: get a terminal execution_id, then in the GUI prompt type `report <execution_id>`. The render block should appear under the textual report.

Verify in the browser: (a) the alert pill renders green with success icon, (b) the markdown section shows ## heading, list items, inline code, and the link, (c) the button is visible and clickable, (d) clicking the button re-invokes `report <execution_id>` (handleWidgetEvent intercepts key=='command').
Capture screenshots of: (i) the rendered widget under the report output, (ii) the prompt history after clicking the button (should append a fresh report entry).
If any widget renders as 'unsupported widget' the JSON shape is wrong — capture exactly what the prompt received and dump it in the result file.
5_widget_smoke_via_synthetic_playbook.verify: All four widget kinds (column, alert, markdown, button) render correctly. Button click re-runs the report command. No console errors. Screenshots captured.

6_lint_only_smoke_for_unhandled_widgets.objective: Confirm graceful degradation: an unknown widget type does NOT crash the prompt.
spec: Modify the smoke playbook (or add a second one) to emit `render: {type: 'app:nonexistent', args: {hello: 'world'}}`.
Run + report. The renderer should display the 'unsupported widget' fallback surface (small div with 'unsupported widget: app:nonexistent' and a JSON preview of the args).
Verify the prompt did NOT crash, no React error overlay, no white screen.
Capture a screenshot of the unsupported-widget surface.
6_lint_only_smoke_for_unhandled_widgets.verify: Unsupported widget falls back gracefully with JSON preview. No console errors beyond the WidgetRenderer's own console.warn (which is expected and intentional).

7_bump_ai_meta_pointers.objective: Bump repos/gui and repos/docs submodule pointers in ai-meta.
spec: `cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/gui repos/docs`.
`git add repos/gui repos/docs`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump gui + docs for chatui-aligned widget renderer`.
Don't push ai-meta — user pushes that themselves.
7_bump_ai_meta_pointers.verify: ai-meta has 1 commit ahead of origin with the two submodule pointer bumps.

expected_state.description: GUI PR merged, docs PR merged, new gui image deployed to local kind, widget render smoke validated end-to-end with a synthetic playbook, unsupported-widget fallback verified, ai-meta submodule pointers bumped (staged).
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; CTX=$(kubectl config current-context 2>/dev/null || echo none); echo "context: $CTX"; GUI_TAG=$(kubectl --context $CTX -n noetl get deploy/gui -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/.*://' || echo none); echo "gui tag: $GUI_TAG"; LATEST_GUI_TAG=$(cd repos/gui && git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo none); echo "latest gui tag: $LATEST_GUI_TAG"; WIDGET_DIR=0; if [ -d repos/gui/src/components/widgets ] && [ -f repos/gui/src/components/widgets/WidgetRenderer.tsx ]; then WIDGET_DIR=1; fi; echo "widgets dir present: $WIDGET_DIR"; WIDGET_DOC=0; if [ -f repos/docs/docs/gui/widgets.md ]; then WIDGET_DOC=1; fi; echo "widgets.md in docs: $WIDGET_DOC"; if [ "$GUI_TAG" = "$LATEST_GUI_TAG" ] && [ "$WIDGET_DIR" -eq 1 ] && [ "$WIDGET_DOC" -eq 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: WIDGET_RENDERER_DEPLOYED'; else echo "GOAL: NOT_YET gui_tag=$GUI_TAG/$LATEST_GUI_TAG widgets=$WIDGET_DIR doc=$WIDGET_DOC"; fi

denied_tools: Modifying noetl repo or any noetl Python (no noetl-side changes in round 2 — widgets are GUI-side only)
Adding Widget kind to noetl Python (architectural directive — widgets are GUI/data only)
Modifying anything in references/chatui (read-only submodule per AGENTS.md)
Cutting a noetl release (irrelevant; no noetl changes)
Adjusting Claude's TypeScript widget code beyond obvious typo fixes — Claude designed the implementation to mirror chatui contracts; if there's a real issue, capture in result file and stop
Switching kind from Podman to Colima or Docker (per AGENTS.md, Podman machine only for local kind)
preferred_tools: cd repos/gui && npm install && npx tsc --noEmit
cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build
git apply / git commit -F - / git push (topic branches in repos/gui and repos/docs only)
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch / gh release view
noetl exec noetl/lifecycle/bump_image --runtime local (gui-only redeploy fallback)
Browser screenshot via DevTools or operator tooling for the smoke test
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260508-064720-deploy-widget-renderer-round-2-local.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_typecheck_and_build: {tsc_status, build_status, ts_errors, bundle_size_delta?}, 2_open_and_merge_gui_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha, gui_release_tag}, 3_open_and_merge_docs_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha, docs_build_clean: bool}, 4_local_kind_redeploy: {kind_context, gui_tag_before, gui_tag_after, rollout_status}, 5_widget_smoke_via_synthetic_playbook: {synthetic_playbook_path, execution_id, widget_kinds_rendered: ['app:column', 'app:alert', 'app:markdown', 'app:button'], button_click_reran_command: bool, screenshots: [...], any_console_errors: [...]}, 6_lint_only_smoke_for_unhandled_widgets: {unsupported_fallback_visible: bool, no_react_overlay: bool, screenshot}, 7_bump_ai_meta_pointers: {gui_sha, docs_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Round 2.x: surface extractAgentRender in mcp/k8s/call command paths, not just report', 'Round 2.x: feed app:form submit values into a follow-up run <playbook> payload', 'Round 3: terminal-window unification with token cursor', 'Round 4: token-graph related-items', 'Round 5+: cross-cluster token addressability']}. If verdict GREEN, append a one-paragraph entry to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md noting the widget renderer milestone.
